reviewFocus: Check that the packet exposes only opaque source handles, redacted evidence notes, and reusable finding candidates rather than raw private-domain truth.
Check that reusable findings stay generic enough for open-source maintenance and do not encode project names, repository facts, actor identity, or private-domain routing.
Check that stable finding keys are used only when they stay generic across runs and do not depend on private naming or caller-owned identifiers.
Check that the bridge output remains advisory and caller-owned instead of implying queue writes, priority assignment, approval, or execution authority.
outOfScope: Resolving the underlying sources, verifying private artifacts, or reconstructing the original domain lane.
Assigning priority, writing queue state, changing ownership, or approving downstream execution.
Inferring repository names, tracker identifiers, account names, private implementation notes, or private policy from the redacted packet.
